completed: Auth system — all 5 endpoints fully implemented and wired
Department API — GET, POST, GET/:id, PATCH/:id (admin/hod scoped, cached)
Subject API — GET, POST, GET/:id, PATCH/:id, DELETE/:id (soft delete, cached)
Class API — all 10 endpoints (CRUD, subject assignments, clone-subjects, class-teacher)
Faculty API — all 6 endpoints (CRUD + GET/:id/classes, temp password generation)
Student API — all 9 endpoints (CRUD, mentor, electives add/update/remove)
All route files: auth, department, subject, class, faculty, student
app.js — all 6 route groups mounted at correct base paths
Models updated: Faculty (employeeId, phone, indexes), Department (no enum, indexes), Admin (12-round), Student (indexes), Subject (isActive, indexes), Class (proper subdoc, indexes)
pending: Batch API — GET, POST /initiate, GET /:batchId, GET /:batchId/students/:studentId, PATCH /:batchId/close, POST /:batchId/students, DELETE /:batchId/faculty/:facultyId
Approvals API — GET /pending, GET /history, POST /approve, POST /mark-due, PATCH /:approvalId
Import API — preview/commit for students, faculty, electives, mentors + template download

decisions.decision: nds_token httpOnly cookie (not Authorization header) as per API guide §2
decisions.trade_offs: Requires credentials:true on CORS; cannot be read by frontend JS (by design)

decisions.decision: All queries use compound indexes; node-cache for hot paths; no unbounded .find() calls
decisions.trade_offs: node-cache is per-instance (not shared across replicas) — acceptable for M0

decisions.decision: bcrypt with 12 rounds (raised from 10) for stronger security
decisions.trade_offs: ~300ms per hash; no visible UX impact at this scale

decisions.decision: Any dept name can be created via API; UI must enforce consistent casing
Two separate lean queries (classTeacher + subjectAssignments) instead of aggregation pipeline to stay M0-safe
decisions.trade_offs: Two DB round-trips vs one aggregation; acceptable for list size
